stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • African American women played key roles in the labor movement, including in the 1955 Montgomery bus boycott.
  • Civil rights organizer Jo Ann Robinson, an English professor, helped draft and circulate the boycott's first call to action.
  • Civil rights activist Mary Fair Burks led a women's political council that helped sustain the boycott.
  • Robinson worked behind the scenes to mobilize ministers and printers.
  • Burks was one of the boycott's most visible public spokespeople at community rallies.
stem: The student wants to compare the two women's contributions to the Montgomery bus boycott. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Robinson and Burks contributed to the boycott in different ways; Burks, for example, addressed community rallies as a public spokesperson.
Robinson was an English professor who helped draft the call to action, while Burks led a women's political council.
Many African American women, including Robinson and Burks, fought for civil rights, but only one drafted the first call to action.
Although Robinson worked behind the scenes to mobilize ministers and printers, Burks was one of the boycott's most visible public spokespeople at community rallies.

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer because it directly compares the two women's contributions: one behind the scenes, the other publicly visible.

Choice A gestures at differences but only develops one side. Choice B describes their roles without a comparative frame. Choice C generalizes about many women rather than comparing these two.
